title: Rightmove Sued for £1.5 Billion by UK Estate Agent Coalition
lead: A coalition of UK estate agencies has filed a £1.5 billion class-action lawsuit against Rightmove, alleging the portal abused its market-dominant position to impose excessive subscription fees. The claimants represent approximately 4,000 agencies and argue that Rightmove's pricing has increased by 50% over five years. The case is being handled by competition law specialist Hausfeld.

title: Home search is going AI-first with Realtor.com as attention gets harder to earn
lead: Realtor.com has overhauled its core search experience, making AI-driven intent parsing the primary discovery mechanism. Users no longer begin with a filter panel; instead, a conversational interface interprets buyer preferences dynamically. The platform has simultaneously launched a native integration with ChatGPT for property search across all devices.

title: Opendoor acquires Doma's closing, escrow and title technology stack
lead: Opendoor has completed the acquisition of the technology assets of Doma, including its AI-driven title insurance platform and escrow automation suite valued at approximately $150 million. Opendoor says the tools will be integrated into its Sell+Close product and made available to third-party agents within 12 months.

title: CoStar Group Launches Transformative AI Experience on Homes.com
lead: CoStar Group unveiled a redesigned Homes.com powered by generative AI that lets users search using natural language descriptions. The system can answer questions about neighborhoods and commute times directly from the search interface. CoStar CEO Andy Florance said the launch marks 'the end of filter-based search as the primary discovery mechanism'.

title: Scout24 acquires Spanish online real estate platforms Fotocasa and Habitaclia
lead: Scout24 has agreed to acquire Fotocasa and Habitaclia from Adevinta for €620 million. The two platforms together represent Spain's number two and number three real estate portals with a combined 12 million monthly visitors. The deal is expected to close in Q3 2026 pending EU regulatory approval.

title: NWMLS files federal lawsuit against Compass over systematic pocket listing practices
lead: Northwest Multiple Listing Service has filed a federal lawsuit against Compass, alleging the brokerage systematically withheld properties from the MLS to favour internal transactions. NWMLS argues the practice harms sellers by reducing competitive buyer exposure. The suit seeks injunctive relief and unspecified damages.

title: Zillow launches Zillow Preview to bring pre-market home listings into the open
lead: Zillow introduced Zillow Preview, a new listing category that allows sellers to test market interest before officially listing. Properties are visible to shoppers but excluded from third-party syndication. Participating brokerages can opt-in sellers starting April 1.

title: Homes.com Expands Pre-Market Listings Program Nationwide
lead: Homes.com has extended its pre-market listing feature to all 50 US states after a six-month pilot in 12 markets. The program allows sellers to list as 'coming soon' and receive buyer inquiries before the home appears on the MLS. Agents can see buyer interest up to 30 days before the official listing date.

title: Czech Regulator Forces Sreality Pricing Reset After Abuse of Dominance Finding
lead: The Czech Office for the Protection of Competition ruled that Sreality must reduce subscription prices for real estate agencies by 35% within 90 days. The regulator found that Sreality leveraged its market position to charge supra-competitive rates. Seznam avoided direct fines by agreeing voluntarily.

title: Zumper CEO to Transition to Boardroom, CRO Steps Up
lead: Zumper founder and CEO Anthemos Georgiades announced he will move to the company's board of directors after ten years. Chief Revenue Officer Matt Curtis has been appointed President effective July 1. The company said the transition is part of a planned evolution toward profitability.

title: CoStar eliminates Domain's Australian strategy, product and tech teams
lead: CoStar Group has dissolved the strategy, product, and technology divisions at Domain. The functions will be absorbed into CoStar's global team in the US. Approximately 120 staff in Sydney and Melbourne are affected.

title: New data shows AI search adoption by homebuyers climbing fast
lead: A survey of 2,400 US homebuyers found that 38% used an AI-assisted search tool during their most recent purchase, up from 11% in 2024. Among buyers under 35, adoption reached 61%. Respondents rated natural language search as significantly less frustrating than filter-based alternatives.

title: Apartments.com Releases Multifamily Rent Growth Report for March 2026
lead: Apartments.com published its monthly Multifamily Rent Growth Report showing that US rental price increases have decelerated for the third consecutive quarter. The national median rent growth rate fell to 1.2% year-over-year, down from 3.8% in the same period last year. Sun Belt markets showed the sharpest slowdowns.

title: Zillow iOS app updated to version 2026.25.0 with bug fixes
lead: Zillow released version 2026.25.0 of its iOS application. The release notes describe minor bug fixes and performance improvements. No new features or UI changes were announced.

title: Rightmove iOS updated to version 10.38 with improved list management
lead: Rightmove released version 10.38 of its iOS app. The update includes improvements to the save and remove functionality for property shortlists. No major product or feature changes are described in the release notes.

title: French Portal Rivalry Intensifies as Bien'ici, SeLoger, and Leboncoin Trade Claims
lead: France's leading real estate portals are engaged in a public dispute over traffic rankings and lead quality. SeLoger published data claiming a 15% lead quality advantage over Bien'ici, while Leboncoin accused both of inflating traffic figures. The rivalry has intensified since Bien'ici relaunched with a new pricing structure in January.

title: Property Finder raises $400M, pledges product depth over geographic expansion
lead: Property Finder, the leading UAE real estate portal, closed a $400 million growth equity round led by KKR. CEO Michael Lahyani said capital will be invested in verified listings, AI valuation tools, and mortgage integrations rather than new geographies. The company declined to disclose its current valuation.

title: Why Data, Not AI, Will Decide the Future of Property Search
lead: OnlineMarketplaces editor Simon Baker argues in a long-form piece that the portals with the most comprehensive property data will win the AI search race, not those with the most advanced language models. He cites CoStar's data moat and Zillow's Zestimate history as evidence that AI is only as good as the underlying data it draws on.

title: Redfin and Opendoor launch joint platform letting sellers compare instant offer vs. traditional listing
lead: Redfin and Opendoor announced a data-sharing partnership presenting sellers with a traditional listing recommendation and an Opendoor cash offer side-by-side in a single interface. The companies will share lead data and a revenue percentage from completed transactions. The integration goes live across 25 markets in May 2026.

title: CoStar Group Completes Acquisition of Visual Lease for $272 Million
lead: CoStar Group completed the acquisition of Visual Lease, a lease accounting software company used by corporate real estate teams. The acquisition extends CoStar's platform from market intelligence into enterprise lease management. Visual Lease serves over 1,500 corporate clients managing more than 500,000 leases.

title: CoStar Accuses Zillow of Ongoing IP Infringement in Expanded Amended Complaint
lead: CoStar Group filed an amended lawsuit against Zillow expanding IP infringement claims to include 9,000 additional property photographs and data allegedly copied from LoopNet. Legal observers say the expansion signals a strategy of exhausting Zillow through protracted litigation over data and image rights.

title: Rightmove introduces new three-tier subscription model for estate agencies
lead: Rightmove unveiled a redesigned subscription structure replacing the flat monthly fee with three tiers: Essential, Professional, and Enterprise. The Enterprise tier at £2,400 per month per branch includes priority placement, AI market appraisal, and enhanced analytics. Rightmove says the change better aligns pricing with value delivered.

title: Compass reports record revenue, cites private listing network as primary growth driver
lead: Compass reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.45 billion, up 18% year-over-year. The company cited its exclusive private listing network accounting for 12% of total transaction volume. Average days-on-market for private listings is 40% lower than comparable MLS listings in the same markets.

title: REA Group launches guaranteed floor-price program for Australian home sellers
lead: REA Group's realestate.com.au introduced a program allowing sellers to lock in a minimum sale price backed by institutional buyers. If the property fails to sell above the floor price within 60 days, an institutional buyer purchases it at that price and REA earns a transaction fee. Launches in Melbourne and Sydney first.

title: Dubizzle Group completes full property transaction platform rollout in Dubai
lead: Dubizzle Group completed the rollout of its end-to-end property transaction platform in Dubai, enabling buyers and sellers to complete documentation, title transfer, and mortgage application entirely through the platform. The company processed over 200 complete transactions in the pilot phase with an NPS of 68.
